title: Search and rescue helicopter statistics: year ending March 2025
body: " Our statistical practice is regulated by the Office for Statistics Regulation (OSR). OSR sets the standards of trustworthiness, quality and value in the Code of Practice for Statistics that all producers of official statistics should adhere to. You are welcome to contact us directly by emailing search and rescue helicopter statistics with any comments about how we meet these standards.   Statistics on civilian search and rescue helicopter (SARH) activity in the United Kingdom, based on details of taskings recorded by the Aeronautical Rescue Co-ordination Centre (ARCC).  For the year ending March 2025:    there were 2,849 civilian SARH taskings   Prestwick base was the busiest of the 10 bases, responding to 450 taskings   there were 1,342 people rescued and 264 assisted across all taskings    There was a 4% increase in taskings compared to the year ending March 2024. The majority of taskings were classified as rescue or recovery (1,372), followed by search (622), support (554) and pre-arranged transfers (301).  Interactive dashboard  Explore the data via our interactive search and rescue helicopter statistics dashboard covering SARH taskings from April 2015 onwards.  Notes and guidance  Notes and definitions and guidance about quality of these statistics is available.  Contact us  Search and rescue helicopter statistics     Email SARH.stats@dft.gov.uk     Media enquiries 0300 7777 878       To hear more about DfT statistical publications as they are released, follow us on X at DfTstats.  "
